crypto.h(3) Library Functions Manual crypto.h(3)

NAME

crypto.h - Server cryptographic operations and per-client handshake management.

SYNOPSIS

Functions


int server_crypto_init (void)
int server_crypto_handshake (client_info_t *client)
bool crypto_server_is_ready (const char *client_id)
const crypto_context_t * crypto_server_get_context (const char *client_id)
int crypto_server_encrypt_packet (const char *client_id, const uint8_t *plaintext, size_t plaintext_len, uint8_t *ciphertext, size_t ciphertext_size, size_t *ciphertext_len)
int crypto_server_decrypt_packet (const char *client_id, const uint8_t *ciphertext, size_t ciphertext_len, uint8_t *plaintext, size_t plaintext_size, size_t *plaintext_len)
void crypto_server_cleanup_client (const char *client_id)

Detailed Description

Server cryptographic operations and per-client handshake management.

Definition in file src/server/crypto.h.

Function Documentation

void crypto_server_cleanup_client (const char * client_id)

Cleanup crypto resources for a specific client

Parameters

client_id Client ID to cleanup crypto for

Definition at line 794 of file src/server/crypto.c.

794                                                          {
795   client_info_t *client = find_client_by_id(client_id);
796   if (!client) {
797     return;
798   }
799 
800   if (client->crypto_initialized) {
801     crypto_handshake_destroy(&client->crypto_handshake_ctx);
802     client->crypto_initialized = false;
803     log_debug("Crypto handshake cleaned up for client %s", client_id);
804   }
805 }




References client_info::crypto_handshake_ctx,
    crypto_handshake_destroy(), client_info::crypto_initialized,
    find_client_by_id(), and log_debug.






int
  crypto_server_decrypt_packet (const char * client_id, const uint8_t *
  ciphertext, size_t ciphertext_len, uint8_t * plaintext, size_t
  plaintext_size, size_t * plaintext_len)


Decrypt a received packet from a specific client




Parameters


client_id Client ID that sent the packet


ciphertext Encrypted data to decrypt


ciphertext_len Length of encrypted data


plaintext Output buffer for decrypted data


plaintext_size Size of output buffer


plaintext_len Output length of decrypted data


Returns


0 on success, -1 on failure


Definition at line 778 of file
  src/server/crypto.c.


779                                                                                                    {
780   client_info_t *client = find_client_by_id(client_id);
781   if (!client) {
782     return -1;
783   }
784 
785   return crypto_decrypt_packet_or_passthrough(&client->crypto_handshake_ctx, crypto_server_is_ready(client_id),
786                                               ciphertext, ciphertext_len, plaintext, plaintext_size, plaintext_len);
787 }






References crypto_decrypt_packet_or_passthrough(),
    client_info::crypto_handshake_ctx, crypto_server_is_ready(),
    and find_client_by_id().


Referenced by process_encrypted_packet().






int
  crypto_server_encrypt_packet (const char * client_id, const uint8_t *
  plaintext, size_t plaintext_len, uint8_t * ciphertext, size_t
  ciphertext_size, size_t * ciphertext_len)


Encrypt a packet for transmission to a specific client




Parameters


client_id Client ID to encrypt for


plaintext Plaintext data to encrypt


plaintext_len Length of plaintext data


ciphertext Output buffer for encrypted data


ciphertext_size Size of output buffer


ciphertext_len Output length of encrypted data


Returns


0 on success, -1 on failure


Definition at line 756 of file
  src/server/crypto.c.


757                                                                                                       {
758   client_info_t *client = find_client_by_id(client_id);
759   if (!client) {
760     return -1;
761   }
762 
763   return crypto_encrypt_packet_or_passthrough(&client->crypto_handshake_ctx, crypto_server_is_ready(client_id),
764                                               plaintext, plaintext_len, ciphertext, ciphertext_size, ciphertext_len);
765 }






References crypto_encrypt_packet_or_passthrough(),
    client_info::crypto_handshake_ctx, crypto_server_is_ready(),
    and find_client_by_id().






const
  crypto_context_t * crypto_server_get_context (const char *
  client_id)


Get crypto context for encryption/decryption for a specific
  client




Parameters


client_id Client ID to get context for


Returns


crypto context or NULL if not ready


Definition at line 732 of file
  src/server/crypto.c.


732                                                                          {
733   if (!crypto_server_is_ready(client_id)) {
734     return NULL;
735   }
736 
737   client_info_t *client = find_client_by_id(client_id);
738   if (!client) {
739     return NULL;
740   }
741 
742   return crypto_handshake_get_context(&client->crypto_handshake_ctx);
743 }






References client_info::crypto_handshake_ctx,
    crypto_handshake_get_context(), crypto_server_is_ready(), and
    find_client_by_id().


Referenced by add_client().






bool crypto_server_is_ready (const char * client_id)


Check if crypto handshake is ready for a specific client




Parameters


client_id Client ID to check


Returns


true if encryption is ready, false otherwise


Definition at line 708 of file
  src/server/crypto.c.


708                                                    {
709   if (GET_OPTION(no_encrypt)) {
710     return false;
711   }
712 
713   client_info_t *client = find_client_by_id(client_id);
714   if (!client) {
715     return false;
716   }
717 
718   if (!client->crypto_initialized) {
719     return false;
720   }
721 
722   bool ready = crypto_handshake_is_ready(&client->crypto_handshake_ctx);
723   return ready;
724 }






References client_info::crypto_handshake_ctx,
    crypto_handshake_is_ready(), client_info::crypto_initialized,
    find_client_by_id(), and GET_OPTION.


Referenced by crypto_server_decrypt_packet(),
    crypto_server_encrypt_packet(), crypto_server_get_context(),
    and process_encrypted_packet().






int
  server_crypto_handshake (client_info_t * client)


Perform crypto handshake with client




Parameters


client Client info structure


Returns


0 on success, -1 on failure


Definition at line 182 of file
  src/server/crypto.c.


182                                                    {
183   if (!client) {
184     FATAL(ERROR_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE, "Client is NULL for crypto handshake");
185     return -1;
186   }
187 
188   // Only skip handshake if BOTH encryption and authentication are disabled
189   bool no_encrypt = GET_OPTION(no_encrypt);
190   bool no_auth = GET_OPTION(no_auth);
191 
192   if (no_encrypt && no_auth) {
193     log_debug("Crypto handshake skipped (ACIP_CRYPTO_NONE: both encryption and authentication disabled)");
194     return 0;
195   }
196 
197   // Initialize crypto context for this specific client
198   const options_t *opts = options_get();
199   const char *password = opts && opts->password[0] != '\0' ? opts->password : "";
200 
201   // Create debug name with client_id
202   char crypto_name[64];
203   SAFE_SNPRINTF(crypto_name, sizeof(crypto_name), "crypto_client_%s", client->client_id);
204 
205   int init_result;
206   if (strlen(password) > 0) {
207     // Password provided - use password-based encryption (even if SSH key is also provided)
208     log_debug("SERVER_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE: Using password-based encryption");
209     init_result = crypto_handshake_init_with_password(crypto_name, &client->crypto_handshake_ctx, true,
210                                                       password); // true = server
211   } else {
212     // Server has SSH key - use standard initialization
213     log_debug("SERVER_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE: Using passwordless-based encryption");
214     init_result = crypto_handshake_init(crypto_name, &client->crypto_handshake_ctx, true); // true = server
215   }
216 
217   if (init_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
218     FATAL(init_result, "Failed to initialize crypto handshake for client %u", client->client_id);
219     return -1;
220   }
221   // Do NOT set crypto_initialized here - it must be set after handshake completes
222   client->crypto_initialized = false;
223 
224   // Set up server keys in the handshake context
225   if (g_server_encryption_enabled && g_server_private_key.type == KEY_TYPE_ED25519) {
226     // Copy server private key to handshake context for signing
227     memcpy(&client->crypto_handshake_ctx.server_private_key, &g_server_private_key, sizeof(private_key_t));
228 
229     // Extract Ed25519 public key from private key for identity
230     client->crypto_handshake_ctx.server_public_key.type = KEY_TYPE_ED25519;
231     memcpy(client->crypto_handshake_ctx.server_public_key.key, g_server_private_key.public_key,
232            ED25519_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE);
233 
234     // SSH key is already configured in the handshake context above
235     // No additional setup needed - SSH keys are used only for authentication
236 
237     log_debug("Server identity keys configured for client %s", client->client_id);
238   }
239 
240   // Set up client whitelist if specified
241   if (g_num_whitelisted_clients > 0) {
242     client->crypto_handshake_ctx.require_client_auth = true;
243     client->crypto_handshake_ctx.client_whitelist = g_client_whitelist;
244     client->crypto_handshake_ctx.num_whitelisted_clients = g_num_whitelisted_clients;
245     log_info("Client whitelist enabled: %zu authorized keys", g_num_whitelisted_clients);
246   }
247 
248   // If --require-server-verify is set, require clients to provide identity keys
249   // This ensures only clients with Ed25519 identity keys can connect
250   if (GET_OPTION(require_server_verify)) {
251     client->crypto_handshake_ctx.require_client_auth = true;
252     log_info("--require-server-verify enabled: clients must provide identity keys");
253   }
254 
255   log_debug("Starting crypto handshake with client %u...", client->client_id);
256 
257   START_TIMER("server_crypto_handshake_client_%s", client->client_id);
258 
259   // Step 0a: Receive client's protocol version
260   log_debug("SERVER_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE: Receiving client protocol version");
261   packet_type_t packet_type;
262   void *payload = NULL;
263   size_t payload_len = 0;
264 
265   log_debug("SERVER_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE: About to receive packet from client %s", client->client_id);
266 
267   // Protect socket access during crypto handshake
268   mutex_lock(&client->client_state_mutex);
269   socket_t socket = client->socket;
270   mutex_unlock(&client->client_state_mutex);
271 
272   if (socket == INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
273     log_debug("SERVER_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE: Socket is invalid for client %u", client->client_id);
274     client->crypto_initialized = false;
275     STOP_TIMER("server_crypto_handshake_client_%s", client->client_id);
276     return -1;
277   }
278 
279   int result = receive_packet(socket, &packet_type, &payload, &payload_len);
280   log_debug("SERVER_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE: Received packet from client %u: result=%d, type=%u", client->client_id, result,
281             packet_type);
282 
283   // Handle client disconnection gracefully
284   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
285     log_info("Client %s disconnected during crypto handshake (connection error)", client->client_id);
286     if (payload) {
287       buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_len);
288     }
289     client->crypto_initialized = false;
290     STOP_TIMER("server_crypto_handshake_client_%s", client->client_id);
291     return -1; // Return error but don't crash the server
292   }
293 
294   // If client sent something other than PROTOCOL_VERSION, it means client is using --no-encrypt
295   // In this case, we need to store the packet for the caller to process and skip the handshake
296   if (packet_type != PACKET_TYPE_PROTOCOL_VERSION) {
297     log_info("Client %u sent packet type %u instead of PROTOCOL_VERSION - using unencrypted mode", client->client_id,
298              packet_type);
299 
300     // Store this packet in the client structure so the caller can process it
301     // This is a bit of a hack, but necessary to preserve the packet we already received
302     client->pending_packet_type = packet_type;
303     client->pending_packet_payload = payload;
304     client->pending_packet_length = payload_len;
305 
306     // Mark crypto as not initialized (no encryption)
307     client->crypto_initialized = false;
308     STOP_TIMER("server_crypto_handshake_client_%s", client->client_id);
309     return 0; // Success - just no encryption
310   }
311 
312   if (payload_len != sizeof(protocol_version_packet_t)) {
313     log_error("Invalid protocol version packet size: %zu, expected %zu", payload_len,
314               sizeof(protocol_version_packet_t));
315     buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_len);
316     client->crypto_initialized = false;
317     STOP_TIMER("server_crypto_handshake_client_%s", client->client_id);
318     return -1;
319   }
320 
321   protocol_version_packet_t client_version;
322   memcpy(&client_version, payload, sizeof(protocol_version_packet_t));
323   log_debug("SERVER_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE: About to free payload for client %s", client->client_id);
324   buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_len);
325   log_debug("SERVER_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE: Payload freed for client %s", client->client_id);
326 
327   // Convert from network byte order
328   uint16_t client_proto_version = NET_TO_HOST_U16(client_version.protocol_version);
329   uint16_t client_proto_revision = NET_TO_HOST_U16(client_version.protocol_revision);
330 
331   uint8_t client_mode = client_version.supports_encryption;
332   log_info("Client %s protocol version: %u.%u (crypto mode: 0x%02x)", client->client_id, client_proto_version,
333            client_proto_revision, client_mode);
334 
335   log_debug("SERVER_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE: About to check encryption support for client %u", client->client_id);
336 
337   // Reject ACIP_CRYPTO_NONE (plaintext only) - at minimum, ACIP handshake must run
338   if (client_mode == ACIP_CRYPTO_NONE) {
339     log_error("Client %s does not support any crypto protocol", client->client_id);
340     log_info("Client %s disconnected - crypto protocol required", client->client_id);
341     client->crypto_initialized = false;
342     STOP_TIMER("server_crypto_handshake_client_%s", client->client_id);
343     return -1; // Return error but don't crash the server
344   }
345 
346   // Store encryption mode for setting encrypt_data flag later
347   client->encrypt_data = ACIP_CRYPTO_HAS_ENCRYPT(client_mode);
348 
349   // Step 0b: Send our protocol version to client
350   log_debug("SERVER_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE: About to prepare server protocol version for client %u", client->client_id);
351   protocol_version_packet_t server_version = {0};
352   log_debug("SERVER_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE: Initialized server_version struct for client %u", client->client_id);
353   server_version.protocol_version = HOST_TO_NET_U16(1);  // Protocol version 1
354   server_version.protocol_revision = HOST_TO_NET_U16(0); // Revision 0
355   server_version.supports_encryption = client_mode;      // Echo client's mode
356   server_version.compression_algorithms = 0;             // No compression for now
357   server_version.compression_threshold = 0;
358   server_version.feature_flags = 0;
359 
360   log_debug("SERVER_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE: About to call send_protocol_version_packet for client %u", client->client_id);
361   result = send_protocol_version_packet(socket, &server_version);
362   log_debug("SERVER_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE: send_protocol_version_packet returned %d for client %u", result,
363             client->client_id);
364   if (result != 0) {
365     log_error("Failed to send protocol version to client %u", client->client_id);
366     log_info("Client %s disconnected - failed to send protocol version", client->client_id);
367     client->crypto_initialized = false;
368     STOP_TIMER("server_crypto_handshake_client_%s", client->client_id);
369     return -1; // Return error but don't crash the server
370   }
371   log_debug("SERVER_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE: Protocol version sent successfully to client %u", client->client_id);
372 
373   // Step 0c: Receive client's crypto capabilities
374   payload = NULL;
375   payload_len = 0;
376 
377   result = receive_packet(socket, &packet_type, &payload, &payload_len);
378   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
379     log_info("Client %s disconnected during crypto capabilities exchange", client->client_id);
380     if (payload) {
381       buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_len);
382     }
383     client->crypto_initialized = false;
384     STOP_TIMER("server_crypto_handshake_client_%s", client->client_id);
385     return -1; // Return error but don't crash the server
386   }
387 
388   if (packet_type != PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_CAPABILITIES) {
389     log_error("Server received packet type 0x%x (decimal %u) - Expected 0x%x (decimal %d)", packet_type, packet_type,
390               PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_CAPABILITIES, PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_CAPABILITIES);
391     log_error("Raw packet type bytes: %02x %02x %02x %02x", (packet_type >> 0) & 0xFF, (packet_type >> 8) & 0xFF,
392               (packet_type >> 16) & 0xFF, (packet_type >> 24) & 0xFF);
393     if (payload) {
394       buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_len);
395     }
396     log_info("Client %s disconnected due to protocol mismatch in crypto capabilities", client->client_id);
397     client->crypto_initialized = false;
398     STOP_TIMER("server_crypto_handshake_client_%s", client->client_id);
399     return -1; // Return error but don't crash the server
400   }
401 
402   if (payload_len != sizeof(crypto_capabilities_packet_t)) {
403     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL,
404               "Invalid crypto capabilities packet size: %zu (expected %zu) - we should disconnect this client",
405               payload_len, sizeof(crypto_capabilities_packet_t));
406     if (payload) {
407       buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_len);
408       payload = NULL;
409     }
410     client->crypto_initialized = false;
411     STOP_TIMER("server_crypto_handshake_client_%s", client->client_id);
412     return -1;
413   }
414 
415   if (!payload) {
416     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL, "Crypto capabilities payload is NULL after validation");
417     log_error("Client %u crypto capabilities payload is NULL after validation - disconnecting", client->client_id);
418     LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Crypto handshake: missing capabilities payload");
419     client->crypto_initialized = false;
420     STOP_TIMER("server_crypto_handshake_client_%s", client->client_id);
421     return -1;
422   }
423 
424   // Copy and validate payload immediately after NULL check to ensure analyzer understands safety
425   crypto_capabilities_packet_t client_caps;
426   memcpy(&client_caps, payload, sizeof(crypto_capabilities_packet_t));
427 
428   // Free the buffer after use
429   buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_len);
430   payload = NULL; // Clear reference to prevent use-after-free
431 
432   // Convert from network byte order
433   uint16_t supported_kex = NET_TO_HOST_U16(client_caps.supported_kex_algorithms);
434   uint16_t supported_auth = NET_TO_HOST_U16(client_caps.supported_auth_algorithms);
435   uint16_t supported_cipher = NET_TO_HOST_U16(client_caps.supported_cipher_algorithms);
436 
437   log_debug("Client %s crypto capabilities: KEX=0x%04x, Auth=0x%04x, Cipher=0x%04x", client->client_id, supported_kex,
438             supported_auth, supported_cipher);
439 
440   // Step 0d: Select crypto algorithms and send parameters to client
441   crypto_parameters_packet_t server_params = {0};
442 
443   // Select algorithms (for now, we only support X25519 + Ed25519 + XSalsa20-Poly1305)
444   server_params.selected_kex = KEX_ALGO_X25519;
445   server_params.selected_cipher =
446       ACIP_CRYPTO_HAS_ENCRYPT(client_mode) ? CIPHER_ALGO_XSALSA20_POLY1305 : CIPHER_ALGO_NONE;
447 
448   // Select authentication algorithm based on server configuration
449   // Note: Password authentication is not a separate algorithm - it's a mode of operation
450   // that affects key derivation. The authentication algorithm refers to signature verification.
451   //
452   // We require Ed25519 authentication if:
453   //   - Server has an identity key (g_server_encryption_enabled AND g_server_private_key is Ed25519)
454   //   - This is needed to send authenticated KEY_EXCHANGE_INIT with identity + signature
455   //   - Client whitelist verification happens during authentication phase, not key exchange
456   if (g_server_encryption_enabled && g_server_private_key.type == KEY_TYPE_ED25519) {
457     // SSH key authentication (Ed25519 signatures) - server has identity key
458     server_params.selected_auth = AUTH_ALGO_ED25519;
459   } else {
460     // No signature-based authentication during key exchange
461     // Client authentication will be required during auth phase if whitelist is enabled
462     server_params.selected_auth = AUTH_ALGO_NONE;
463   }
464 
465   // Set verification flag based on client whitelist
466   server_params.verification_enabled = (g_num_whitelisted_clients > 0) ? 1 : 0;
467 
468   // Set crypto parameters for current algorithms
469   server_params.kex_public_key_size = CRYPTO_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE; // X25519 public key size
470 
471   // Only set auth/signature sizes if we're using authentication
472   if (server_params.selected_auth == AUTH_ALGO_ED25519) {
473     server_params.auth_public_key_size = ED25519_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE; // Ed25519 public key size
474     server_params.signature_size = ED25519_SIGNATURE_SIZE;        // Ed25519 signature size
475   } else {
476     server_params.auth_public_key_size = 0; // No authentication
477     server_params.signature_size = 0;       // No signature
478   }
479 
480   server_params.shared_secret_size = CRYPTO_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE; // X25519 shared secret size
481   server_params.nonce_size = CRYPTO_NONCE_SIZE;              // XSalsa20 nonce size
482   server_params.mac_size = CRYPTO_MAC_SIZE;                  // Poly1305 MAC size
483   server_params.hmac_size = CRYPTO_HMAC_SIZE;                // HMAC-SHA256 size
484 
485   // Send negotiated crypto parameters to client
486   log_debug("SERVER_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE: Sending crypto parameters to client %s", client->client_id);
487   result = send_crypto_parameters_packet(socket, &server_params);
488   if (result != 0) {
489     log_error("Failed to send crypto parameters to client %u", client->client_id);
490     client->crypto_initialized = false;
491     STOP_TIMER("server_crypto_handshake_client_%s", client->client_id);
492     return -1;
493   }
494   log_debug("Server selected crypto for client %s: KEX=%u, Auth=%u, Cipher=%u", client->client_id,
495             server_params.selected_kex, server_params.selected_auth, server_params.selected_cipher);
496 
497   // Set the crypto parameters in the handshake context so that
498   // crypto_handshake_server_start knows parameters were already sent
499   result = crypto_handshake_set_parameters(&client->crypto_handshake_ctx, &server_params);
500   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
501     client->crypto_initialized = false;
502     STOP_TIMER("server_crypto_handshake_client_%s", client->client_id);
503     FATAL(result, "Failed to set crypto parameters for client %u", client->client_id);
504     return -1;
505   }
506 
507   // Step 0.5: Receive CRYPTO_CLIENT_HELLO (multi-key support)
508   // When server has multiple identity keys, client MUST send CLIENT_HELLO to select the correct key
509   // For single-key servers, CLIENT_HELLO is optional (backward compatibility)
510   if (g_num_server_identity_keys > 0) {
511     log_debug("SERVER_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE: Waiting for optional CRYPTO_CLIENT_HELLO from client %u", client->client_id);
512 
513     // Try to receive CLIENT_HELLO packet
514     packet_type_t hello_packet_type;
515     uint8_t *hello_payload = NULL;
516     size_t hello_payload_len = 0;
517 
518     result = receive_packet(socket, &hello_packet_type, (void **)&hello_payload, &hello_payload_len);
519     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
520       log_info("Client %s disconnected during handshake initialization", client->client_id);
521       if (hello_payload) {
522         buffer_pool_free(NULL, hello_payload, hello_payload_len);
523       }
524       client->crypto_initialized = false;
525       STOP_TIMER("server_crypto_handshake_client_%s", client->client_id);
526       return -1;
527     }
528 
529     if (hello_packet_type == PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_CLIENT_HELLO) {
530       // Client sent expected server key - select matching key
531       if (hello_payload_len != ED25519_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE) {
532         log_error("Invalid CRYPTO_CLIENT_HELLO size: %zu (expected %d)", hello_payload_len, ED25519_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE);
533         buffer_pool_free(NULL, hello_payload, hello_payload_len);
534         client->crypto_initialized = false;
535         STOP_TIMER("server_crypto_handshake_client_%s", client->client_id);
536         return -1;
537       }
538 
539       const uint8_t *expected_server_key = hello_payload;
540 
541       // Log the expected key for debugging
542       char hex_expected[65];
543       pubkey_to_hex(expected_server_key, hex_expected);
544       log_debug("Client %s expects server key: %s", client->client_id, hex_expected);
545 
546       // Search g_server_identity_keys[] for matching public key
547       bool key_found = false;
548       for (size_t i = 0; i < g_num_server_identity_keys; i++) {
549         if (sodium_memcmp(expected_server_key, g_server_identity_keys[i].public_key, ED25519_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE) == 0) {
550           // Found matching key - use this one for handshake
551           memcpy(&client->crypto_handshake_ctx.server_private_key, &g_server_identity_keys[i], sizeof(private_key_t));
552 
553           char hex_selected[65];
554           pubkey_to_hex(g_server_identity_keys[i].public_key, hex_selected);
555           log_debug("Client %s selected server identity key #%zu: %s", client->client_id, i + 1, hex_selected);
556 
557           key_found = true;
558           break;
559         }
560       }
561 
562       if (!key_found) {
563         // Client requested a key we don't have - reject connection
564         log_error("Client %s requested unknown server key %s - rejecting connection", client->client_id, hex_expected);
565         buffer_pool_free(NULL, hello_payload, hello_payload_len);
566         client->crypto_initialized = false;
567         STOP_TIMER("server_crypto_handshake_client_%s", client->client_id);
568         return -1;
569       }
570 
571       buffer_pool_free(NULL, hello_payload, hello_payload_len);
572     } else {
573       // Client didn't send CLIENT_HELLO
574       // This is ONLY acceptable for single-key servers (backward compatibility)
575       if (g_num_server_identity_keys > 1) {
576         log_error("Client %u must send CRYPTO_CLIENT_HELLO when server has multiple keys (got packet type %u)",
577                   client->client_id, hello_packet_type);
578         buffer_pool_free(NULL, hello_payload, hello_payload_len);
579         client->crypto_initialized = false;
580         STOP_TIMER("server_crypto_handshake_client_%s", client->client_id);
581         return -1;
582       }
583 
584       // Single-key server: Old client didn't send CLIENT_HELLO
585       // Store this packet for later processing (it's probably the first handshake packet)
586       log_debug("Client %u is using old protocol (no CLIENT_HELLO) - using default key", client->client_id);
587 
588       // Store pending packet so we can process it after KEY_EXCHANGE_INIT
589       client->pending_packet_type = hello_packet_type;
590       client->pending_packet_payload = hello_payload;
591       client->pending_packet_length = hello_payload_len;
592     }
593   }
594 
595   // Use the client's persistent transport for handshake (created in add_client before this call).
596   // The transport starts with NULL crypto_ctx; after handshake we set it.
597   acip_transport_t *transport = client->transport;
598   if (!transport) {
599     log_error("No transport available for client %u handshake", client->client_id);
600     client->crypto_initialized = false;
601     STOP_TIMER("server_crypto_handshake_client_%s", client->client_id);
602     return -1;
603   }
604 
605   // Step 1: Send our public key to client
606   log_debug("About to call crypto_handshake_server_start");
607   result = crypto_handshake_server_start(&client->crypto_handshake_ctx, transport);
608   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
609     log_error("Crypto handshake start failed for client %u: %s", client->client_id, asciichat_error_string(result));
610     LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Crypto handshake: failed to send KEY_EXCHANGE_INIT");
611     client->crypto_initialized = false;
612     STOP_TIMER("server_crypto_handshake_client_%s", client->client_id);
613     return -1;
614   }
615 
616   // Step 2: Receive client's public key and send auth challenge
617   {
618     packet_type_t step2_type;
619     uint8_t *step2_payload = NULL;
620     size_t step2_len = 0;
621     int recv_result = receive_packet(socket, &step2_type, (void **)&step2_payload, &step2_len);
622     if (recv_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
623       log_error("Failed to receive KEY_EXCHANGE_RESPONSE from client %u", client->client_id);
624       client->crypto_initialized = false;
625       STOP_TIMER("server_crypto_handshake_client_%s", client->client_id);
626       return -1;
627     }
628     result = crypto_handshake_server_auth_challenge(&client->crypto_handshake_ctx, transport, step2_type, step2_payload,
629                                                     step2_len);
630     buffer_pool_free(NULL, step2_payload, step2_len);
631   }
632   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
633     log_error("Crypto authentication challenge failed for client %u: %s", client->client_id,
634               asciichat_error_string(result));
635     client->crypto_initialized = false;
636     STOP_TIMER("server_crypto_handshake_client_%s", client->client_id);
637     return -1; // Return error to disconnect client gracefully
638   }
639 
640   // Check if handshake completed during auth challenge (no authentication needed)
641   if (client->crypto_handshake_ctx.state == CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_READY) {
642     const char *cid = client->client_id;
643     client->crypto_initialized = true;
644     // Propagate encryption flag to crypto context
645     client->crypto_handshake_ctx.crypto_ctx.encrypt_data = client->encrypt_data;
646     // Set crypto context on the persistent transport now that handshake is complete
647     transport->crypto_ctx = (crypto_context_t *)crypto_handshake_get_context(&client->crypto_handshake_ctx);
648     STOP_TIMER_AND_LOG(debug, 100 * NS_PER_MS_INT, "server_crypto_handshake_client_%s",
649                        "Crypto handshake completed successfully for client %s (no authentication)", cid);
650     return 0;
651   }
652 
653   // Step 3: Receive auth response and complete handshake
654   {
655     packet_type_t step3_type = 0;
656     uint8_t *step3_payload = NULL;
657     size_t step3_len = 0;
658     int recv_result = receive_packet(socket, &step3_type, (void **)&step3_payload, &step3_len);
659     if (recv_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
660       log_info("Client %s disconnected during authentication", client->client_id);
661       client->crypto_initialized = false;
662       STOP_TIMER("server_crypto_handshake_client_%s", client->client_id);
663       return -1;
664     }
665     result = crypto_handshake_server_complete(&client->crypto_handshake_ctx, transport, step3_type, step3_payload,
666                                               step3_len);
667     buffer_pool_free(NULL, step3_payload, step3_len);
668   }
669   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
670     if (result == ERROR_NETWORK || result == ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL || result == ERROR_CRYPTO_AUTH ||
671         result == ERROR_CRYPTO_VERIFICATION || result == ERROR_CRYPTO) {
672       if (result == ERROR_NETWORK) {
673         log_info("Client %s disconnected during authentication", client->client_id);
674       } else {
675         log_error("Crypto authentication failed for client %s: %s", client->client_id, asciichat_error_string(result));
676       }
677       LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Crypto handshake completion failed");
678       client->crypto_initialized = false;
679       STOP_TIMER("server_crypto_handshake_client_%s", client->client_id);
680       return -1;
681     }
682     STOP_TIMER("server_crypto_handshake_client_%s", client->client_id);
683     FATAL(result, "Crypto authentication response failed for client %s", client->client_id);
684     client->crypto_initialized = false;
685     return -1;
686   }
687 
688   const char *cid = client->client_id;
689   client->crypto_initialized = true;
690   // Propagate encryption flag to crypto context
691   client->crypto_handshake_ctx.crypto_ctx.encrypt_data = client->encrypt_data;
692   // Set crypto context on the persistent transport now that handshake is complete
693   transport->crypto_ctx = (crypto_context_t *)crypto_handshake_get_context(&client->crypto_handshake_ctx);
694   STOP_TIMER_AND_LOG(debug, 100 * NS_PER_MS_INT, "server_crypto_handshake_client_%s",
695                      "Crypto handshake completed successfully for client %s", cid);
696 
697   // Send success notification to client (encrypted channel now established)
698   log_info_client(client, "Encryption established - secure channel ready");
699   return 0;
700 }






References ACIP_CRYPTO_HAS_ENCRYPT,
    ACIP_CRYPTO_NONE, ASCIICHAT_OK, AUTH_ALGO_ED25519,
    AUTH_ALGO_NONE,
    crypto_parameters_packet_t::auth_public_key_size,
    buffer_pool_free(), CIPHER_ALGO_NONE,
    CIPHER_ALGO_XSALSA20_POLY1305, client_info::client_id,
    client_info::client_state_mutex,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::client_whitelist,
    protocol_version_packet_t::compression_algorithms,
    protocol_version_packet_t::compression_threshold,
    acip_transport::crypto_ctx,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::crypto_ctx,
    client_info::crypto_handshake_ctx,
    crypto_handshake_get_context(), crypto_handshake_init(),
    crypto_handshake_init_with_password(), CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_READY,
    crypto_handshake_server_auth_challenge(),
    crypto_handshake_server_complete(),
    crypto_handshake_server_start(),
    crypto_handshake_set_parameters(), CRYPTO_HMAC_SIZE,
    client_info::crypto_initialized, CRYPTO_MAC_SIZE,
    CRYPTO_NONCE_SIZE, CRYPTO_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE,
    ED25519_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE, ED25519_SIGNATURE_SIZE,
    client_info::encrypt_data, crypto_context_t::encrypt_data,
    ERROR_CRYPTO, ERROR_CRYPTO_AUTH,
    ERROR_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE, ERROR_CRYPTO_VERIFICATION,
    ERROR_NETWORK, ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL, FATAL,
    protocol_version_packet_t::feature_flags, g_client_whitelist,
    g_num_server_identity_keys, g_num_whitelisted_clients,
    g_server_encryption_enabled, g_server_identity_keys,
    g_server_private_key, GET_OPTION,
    crypto_parameters_packet_t::hmac_size, HOST_TO_NET_U16,
    INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE, KEX_ALGO_X25519,
    crypto_parameters_packet_t::kex_public_key_size,
    public_key_t::key, KEY_TYPE_ED25519, log_debug,
    LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET, log_error, log_info,
    log_info_client, crypto_parameters_packet_t::mac_size,
    mutex_lock, mutex_unlock, NET_TO_HOST_U16,
    crypto_parameters_packet_t::nonce_size, NS_PER_MS_INT,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::num_whitelisted_clients,
    options_get(), PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_CAPABILITIES,
    PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_CLIENT_HELLO, PACKET_TYPE_PROTOCOL_VERSION,
    options_state::password, client_info::pending_packet_length,
    client_info::pending_packet_payload,
    client_info::pending_packet_type,
    protocol_version_packet_t::protocol_revision,
    protocol_version_packet_t::protocol_version, pubkey_to_hex(),
    receive_packet(),
    crypto_handshake_context_t::require_client_auth,
    SAFE_SNPRINTF, crypto_parameters_packet_t::selected_auth,
    crypto_parameters_packet_t::selected_cipher,
    crypto_parameters_packet_t::selected_kex,
    send_crypto_parameters_packet(),
    send_protocol_version_packet(),
    crypto_handshake_context_t::server_private_key,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::server_public_key, SET_ERRNO,
    crypto_parameters_packet_t::shared_secret_size,
    crypto_parameters_packet_t::signature_size,
    client_info::socket, START_TIMER,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::state, STOP_TIMER,
    STOP_TIMER_AND_LOG,
    crypto_capabilities_packet_t::supported_auth_algorithms,
    crypto_capabilities_packet_t::supported_cipher_algorithms,
    crypto_capabilities_packet_t::supported_kex_algorithms,
    protocol_version_packet_t::supports_encryption,
    client_info::transport, public_key_t::type, and
    crypto_parameters_packet_t::verification_enabled.


Referenced by add_client().






int
  server_crypto_init (void )


Initialize server crypto system (global initialization)




Returns


0 on success, -1 on failure


Definition at line 162 of file
  src/server/crypto.c.


162                              {
163   // Only skip handshake if BOTH encryption and authentication are disabled
164   bool no_encrypt = GET_OPTION(no_encrypt);
165   bool no_auth = GET_OPTION(no_auth);
166 
167   if (no_encrypt && no_auth) {
168     log_info("ACIP handshake disabled (both encryption and authentication disabled)");
169     return 0;
170   }
171 
172   log_debug("Server crypto system initialized (per-client contexts will be created on demand)");
173   return 0;
174 }






References GET_OPTION, log_debug, and
    log_info.


Referenced by add_client().
